skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem: Data set M consists of the lengths of 80 fish and has a mean of 22 centimeters. Data set N consists of the lengths of 40 fish and has a mean of 49 centimeters. Data set P consists of the lengths of all 120 fish from data sets M and N. What is the mean, in centimeters, of data set P?
rationale: Total length in M: 80 · 22 = 1,760. Total length in N: 40 · 49 = 1,960. Combined total: 1,760 + 1,960 = 3,720. Combined count: 120. Mean of P: 3,720 / 120 = 31.
